step_by_step_thinking:
Answer:

We are looking for a molecule that has a permanent dipole. A permanent dipole arises when there is an uneven distribution of electron density due to differences in electronegativity between atoms, often leading to polar bonds and a net molecular polarity. 

Water (H₂O) has two highly electronegative oxygen atoms bonded to hydrogen atoms, creating bent geometry and significant bond dipoles that do not cancel—resulting in a net permanent dipole. In contrast, O₂ molecules have identical atoms and nonpolar covalent bonding; helium is monatomic and inert; liquid nitrogen consists of N₂ molecules which are symmetric and nonpolar.
raw_response:
Answer:

We are looking for a molecule that has a permanent dipole. A permanent dipole arises when there is an uneven distribution of electron density due to differences in electronegativity between atoms, often leading to polar bonds and a net molecular polarity. 

Water (H₂O) has two highly electronegative oxygen atoms bonded to hydrogen atoms, creating bent geometry and significant bond dipoles that do not cancel—resulting in a net permanent dipole. In contrast, O₂ molecules have identical atoms and nonpolar covalent bonding; helium is monatomic and inert; liquid nitrogen consists of N₂ molecules which are symmetric and nonpolar.

Answer: D: Water
